rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MARY HA PHU
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #7, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/25/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No ex parte petition was filed to increase bond. (It is unclear how the annual receipts were determined.) (2) Reimbursements to individuals must clearly indicate who the original payee was. [LRSF 14.34 B.] (3) Why are there $300 "Food Reimbursement" amounts paid to De Wen Lu? Does the care home not provide food? (4) Did it really take 1.5 hours to prepare a notice of hearing on 7/17/23? (5) The senior attorney services on 7/21/23 total .4, not 1.00. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 3/15/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The conservator's compensation amounts seem to be mathematically incorrect...)

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF ELIZABETH BUCKLEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 10/17/2023) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) Paragraph 14d of the petition: The Court's 12/06/2021 order denying fees of the conservator and attorneys was not timely appealed or set aside. As such, the order has become final and conclusive on the issue. No legal authority has been provided for the fee request to be reconsidered. 3) Paragraph 24: Was the conservatee survived by issue of her predeceased spouse (PJ Buckley)? If so, they must be identified and receive notice, as they would be the conservatee's heirs. 4) Schedule D to predeath accounting: Allegations must be made re compliance with Probate Code section 2545(c)(2) and CRC 7.1059(b)(17) as to disposition of each item. 5) Schedule A to postdeath accounting: Is the estate required to refund the $86.52 payment to Kaiser? 6) Schedule C to postdeath accounting: Does the estate anticipate receiving a refund of the $6,165 payment from the facility? 7) No bond fees have been paid for 2021 and subsequent years. Does the conservator intend to charge the estate? Note: It should be noted that the petition requests an order discharging the conservator. If the conservator needs continuing authority to perform any act, other than payment of fees, the discharge request should be made separately by ex parte petition. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KAR WAI LEUNG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/27/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) An amended petition was e-filed on 2/1/24 (no courtesy copy has been received by the examiner), but notice was mailed on 1/31/24. [LRSF 14.14 A.] (2) Paragraph 7 of the amended petition still has not been corrected to show the total I&A amount ($503,635.73, not $503,113.96). (3) Selling securities is a change in the form of assets and must be reported even when the accounting has been waived. [CRC 7.550(b).] There were 395 shares of Facebook on the date of death. Why are there only 315 shares of Meta as of 4/30/23? There were 1,265.4744 shares of Huntington "Bancshs" on the date of death, but only 265 shares as on 4/30/23. There were 518.9777 shares of Wells Fargo & Co. on the date of death but only 268.9777 shares as of 4/30/23. What happened to the 10 shares of Twitter? (4) The caption of the pleading fails to disclose that fees for extraordinary services for the attorney are being requested. (5) Nothing has been filed to address the issues with the attorney declaration filed on 8/16/23, which does not meet the requirements of CRC 7.702. Furthermore, there are references to an "assistant", but the time sheets are not clear as to who billed what hours or what the billing party's hourly rate is and if the "assistant" qualifies to bill. (Is s/he an associate attorney or a paralegal?) (6) There is no mention in this amended petition of funeral expenses the administrator seeks to be reimbursed, but the request to pay "herself" $13,072.71 is in the prayer  but not included in the calculation of the net distribution to the heirs. (7) The second $435 filing fee for this petition will not be allowed. If the attorney insists on being reimbursed this second filing fee, an appearance will be required after all remaining issues are addressed. An AMENDMENT to the amended petition must be filed. Notice of the amendment and the amended petition must be served after the amendment is e-filed. The attorney fee declaration must be supplemented. Deadline for providing courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues is 3/18/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Market values should NOT be part of the proposed distribution. SHARES of securities are what the Court will order to be distributed.)
